data.title: Dutch Bros Stock Soars On Q4 Beat, Expansion Momentum
data.description:
Dutch Bros Inc. (NYSE:BROS) shares are trading higher Friday after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results.

  • Dutch Bros shares are climbing with conviction. What’s driving BROS stock higher?

Double Beat, Performance Metrics And Expansion Momentum

Dutch Bros reported adjusted earnings per share of 17 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 10 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $443.61 million, beating the consensus estimate of $424.75 million.

Total revenue increased 29.4% year over year to $443.6 million from $342.8 million in the same period of 2024.

Systemwide same shop sales rose 7.7%, while systemwide same shop transactions increased 5.4% compared to the prior-year quarter. Company-operated same shop sales grew 9.7% and company-operated same shop transactions climbed 7.6% year over year.

The company opened 55 new shops during the quarter, 52 of which were company-operated, expanding across 17 states.

CFO Josh Guenser said confidence in achieving the company's goal of 2,029 shops by 2029 has never been higher, citing expansion into seven contiguous states and record average unit volumes of $2.1 million in 2025.

Dutch Bros sees fiscal-year revenue between $2.00 billion and $2.03 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $2.03 billion.

Mixed Short-Term Trend; Bearish Momentum Signals

Currently, Dutch Bros stock is trading approximately 4.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 0.5% below its 50-day SMA, indicating a mixed short-term trend. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 39.17%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, which suggests some recovery potential.

The RSI is at 33.22, indicating neutral momentum, while the MACD is below its signal line, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock. This combination of technical indicators reflects a cautious outlook, as the stock navigates through a challenging market environment.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that traders should remain vigilant for potential shifts in market sentiment.

  • Key Resistance: $64.00
  • Key Support: $58.50

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Dutch Bros, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 21.3) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Growth: Strong (Score: 89.23) — Indicates robust growth potential.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 8.11) — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Dutch Bros’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the strong growth score indicates potential, the weak value and momentum scores suggest caution for investors considering entry points.

Dutch Bros Stock Soars Higher

BROS Price Action: At the time of publication, Dutch Bros stock is trading 5.45% higher at $53.59, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Plug Power Stock Edges Higher As Shareholders Approve Share Increase, Avoid Reverse Split
data.description:
• Plug Power stock is building positive momentum. What’s driving PLUG shares up?

Capital Flexibility, Strategic Growth

For shareholders, the vote is about more than mechanics. Plug is racing to fund fuel-cell plants, expand hydrogen production and serve industrial and data-center customers.

Extra shares give management flexibility to raise cash in smaller, staged offerings instead of being pushed into a reverse split that could have scared off institutions and increased volatility.

What Investors Will Watch Next

The authorization also buys Plug time with creditors and suppliers. A thicker equity cushion can help renegotiate financing, secure long-term supply deals and keep strategic partners engaged while the company works to prove its business model and move toward positive cash flow.

Still, the decision sharpens the trade-off for holders. Future stock sales will dilute existing investors, and management will now be judged on how carefully it taps capital markets and whether new funds flow into projects that grow revenue and narrow losses.

Plug Power Technical Analysis Update

Currently, Plug Power is trading 15.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 25.2% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a challenging short-term outlook.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 3.41% and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing volatility.

The RSI is at 38.38, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $2.00
  • Key Support: $1.50

March 2 Earnings Preview

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on March 2.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 10 cents (Up from $-1.65 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $218.70 million (Up from $191.47 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $2.38. Recent analyst moves include:

  • TD Cowen: Downgraded to Hold (Lowers Target to $2.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Clear Street: Upgraded to Buy (Lowers Target to $3.00) (Dec. 31, 2025)
  • Canaccord Genuity: Hold (Maintains Target to $2.50) (Nov. 20, 2025)

Top ETF Exposure

  • Global X Hydrogen ETF (NASDAQ:HYDR): 12.33% Weight
  • Direxion Hydrogen ETF (NYSE:HJEN): 6.90% Weight

Significance: Because PLUG carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

PLUG Shares Edge Higher Friday

PLUG Price Action: Plug Power shares were up 1.65% at $1.85 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
